title: Private Credit: Still quite far from a 2008-event
section_path: Private Credit: Still quite far from a 2008-event
section_path_text: Private Credit: Still quite far from a 2008-event
summary:
Main
Private Credit becoming a systemic risk capable of destabilising broader asset classes is not a central scenario, however, it warrants close monitoring. The market itself is not particularly large, $1.5 trillion, with approximately $100-$225 billion of assets considered at risk. As a proportion of total bank lending, exposure is estimated at roughly 7.5%-8.0% of aggregate loans.
